title: Design mintability rules tied to network activity
markdown:
## Goal

Design the rules under which new B3ND tokens can be minted (if mintability is adopted), including verifiable conditions tied to network activity, maximum inflation rates, and governance controls. This is a contingency design -- the base case is fixed supply, but mintability may be needed if the reward pool depletes before the network is self-sustaining.

## Background

Fixed supply is simpler and more predictable, but it has a failure mode: if the reward pool runs out before gas fees generate enough revenue, validators have no incentive to continue operating. Mintability provides a safety valve.

Key questions:
- Under what conditions is minting triggered? (Low fund balance? High network activity? Governance vote?)
- How much can be minted? (Maximum annual inflation? Per-epoch cap?)
- Who authorizes minting? (Algorithmic? Governance? Multi-sig?)
- Where do minted tokens go? (Directly to reward pool? Distributed pro-rata to stakers?)
- How is minting verified? (On-chain proof of network activity metrics)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Minting trigger conditions defined: specific, measurable, on-chain verifiable
- [ ] Maximum inflation rate: annual cap (e.g., 5% of total supply per year)
- [ ] Per-epoch minting cap: maximum tokens mintable per epoch
- [ ] Authorization mechanism: algorithmic (auto-triggered by conditions) vs governance (requires vote)
- [ ] Destination of minted tokens: reward pool, stakers, or mixed
- [ ] Verification: how validators verify that minting conditions are met (read on-chain metrics)
- [ ] Sunset clause: conditions under which mintability is disabled permanently
- [ ] Impact analysis: effect of minting on existing token holders, bridge AMM, circulating supply
- [ ] Comparison with other inflationary token models (Ethereum post-merge, Cosmos, Polkadot)

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `docs/book/consensus/tge-supply-modeling.md` (G3-C4-01) for sustainability analysis
2. Create `docs/book/consensus/mintability-rules.md`:
   - Section 1: Rationale
     - Why consider mintability: reward pool depletion risk
     - Why fixed supply is preferred: predictability, no dilution
     - Decision framework: mint only if reward pool < X% and network activity > Y threshold
   - Section 2: Trigger Conditions
     - Condition A: Reward pool balance < 5% of initial allocation
     - Condition B: Network activity (transactions per epoch) above sustainability threshold
     - Both conditions must be met (ensures minting only when needed AND network is active)
   - Section 3: Minting Parameters
     - Annual cap: 2% of total supply
     - Per-epoch cap: annual cap / (365 * epochs_per_day)
     - Destination: 80% to reward pool, 20% to network fund
   - Section 4: Authorization
     - Initially: algorithmic (auto-triggered when conditions met)
     - Future: governance vote required to enable/disable mintability
     - Emergency: multi-sig can pause minting
   - Section 5: Sunset Clause
     - If the fund has been self-sustaining (fee revenue > rewards) for 12 consecutive months, mintability is permanently disabled
     - This ensures minting is a temporary bootstrap mechanism, not a permanent feature
   - Section 6: Impact Analysis
     - Dilution effect on existing holders
     - Effect on bridge AMM (more B3ND supply -> potential peg pressure)
     - Mitigation: minted tokens go to locked pools (reward, fund) not circulating supply

## Files to Create/Modify

- `docs/book/consensus/mintability-rules.md` -- new design document

## Testing

No code tests. Design review.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G3-C4-01 (supply modeling shows whether mintability is needed)
- **Blocks**: Nothing -- this is a contingency design
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Model supply dynamics across three pools (FCAT on Solana, xFCAT on Solana, B3ND on B3nd)
- [ ] Model equilibrium dynamics (bridge AMM peg, protocol locks vs circulating, fund refill, withdrawal pricing)
- [ ] Perform adversarial analysis validation (test deposit drain, xFCAT corner, recycling grinder, scorched earth scenarios with real numbers)
- [ ] Design mintability rules if adopted (verifiable conditions tied to network activity)

---

## D. Product & Apps
